claim: Every applied proposal shall leave a trace entry in the runtime land-log (.runtime/land-log.jsonl).
why: Not yet implemented: no `.runtime/land-log.jsonl` writer exists in this system today (the `.runtime` directory does not exist in the repo). `hotam land` (cmd/hotam/land.go) runs apply-proposal -> gen-spec -> all-violations but writes no runtime land-log, and gate_status is Planned in methodology.Tools (not yet available as a `hotam` CLI command), so nothing reads a land-log to answer the commit-boundary question (R-commit-boundary-checkable, hotam-spec-self). (Previously implemented as a `spec/.runtime/land-log.jsonl` writer recording the verification tier T1/T2 and the test outcome, which gate_status read.) The original WHY stands: without an auditable land-log trace there is no durable record of what actually got verified before landing. PROSE (not ENFORCED): no enforcer exists.
